title: chenolea enchylaenoides
intro: Chenolea enchylaenoides, a plant native to Australia, is a type of shrub or small tree belonging to the family Rutaceae, which also includes citrus and rue plants. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to the presence of limonoids and other bioactive compounds. Chenolea enchylaenoides has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and skin irritations, and is believed to offer health benefits such as immune system support and antioxidant activity. The plant can be prepared as an infusion, tincture, or essential oil, and is often used in herbal remedies and traditional medicine practices.
